You notice mid-session that your client has become tense and very quiet. He is awake, but does not respond to your questions. His breathing is shallow and rapid. What is your best response?
choices: Stop the massage, make eye contact with him, and speak softly until he returns to responsiveness. Then determine whether it is appropriate to continue
Continue working, but begin chatting about light-hearted topics so he can focus on things that are not threatening
Keep working as usual; to stop will only embarrass him and make him more upset
Work more slowly and more deeply in the area where he began to “tune out”—this is a sign that he is close to a much-needed emotional release
questions.correctAnswer: Stop the massage, make eye contact with him, and speak softly until he returns to responsiveness. Then determine whether it is appropriate to continue

questions.correctAnswer: Trying to address a protective muscle splinting pattern prematurely can cause more spasm and more pain

questions.correctAnswer: It is important to work conservatively to avoid overtreatment

questions.correctAnswer: Healthy liver cells are replaced with scar tissue

Kai Zen, a 38-year software engineer has been complaining of chronic tension on his neck and shoulders. He finally booked a massage and the therapist performed a thorough interview and assessment.

The therapist was able to gather more information and found that Kai works long hours, never stretches or exercises. The therapist identified a forward head posture pattern as well as rigid and limited movement during head extension. Client also complained of nagging, dull pain at the base of his head.

During re-assessment after the first session, Kai Zen reports a reduction in tension in pain through his neck and shoulders. He and the therapist identify some improvements to range of motion.

What client education information could the massage therapist off to Kai?
choices: Take regular stretch-breaks from his work.
Ensure Kai knows his behavior could lead to arthritis, nerve damage and additional reduced function when he is older.
Tell him to have his company provide an ergonomic specialist to adapt his work space.
questions.correctAnswer: Take regular stretch-breaks from his work.

questions.question: What are the most reliable factors in developing varicose veins?

Age, gender, smoking, being under weight
History of deep vein thrombosis, weak calf muscles
Wear and tear, gravity, mechanical obstruction
questions.correctAnswer: Wear and tear, gravity, mechanical obstruction

questions.question: What postural deviations would be seen on a client if the rectus abdominis fibers were shortened and unable to completely lengthen:
choices: Spinal extension and a posterior tilt to the pelvis
Spinal flexion and a posterior tilt to the pelvis
Spinal extension and an anterior tilt to the pelvis
Spinal flexion and an anterior tilt to the pelvis
questions.correctAnswer: Spinal flexion and a posterior tilt to the pelvis

questions.question:
What are the common attributes of all Lymphatic Facilitation strokes and techniques?

(Select all that apply)
choices: Strokes are applied in the direction that facilitates normal lymph flow
Lots of lotion or oil must be used
Tissue must be fully released and allowed to "snap back" to its original position between strokes
Light pressure and a slight stretch to the skin
To be effective, stokes must be repeated 15 - 20 times over the same location
correctAnswers: Strokes are applied in the direction that facilitates normal lymph flow
Tissue must be fully released and allowed to "snap back" to its original position between strokes
Light pressure and a slight stretch to the skin

Select a muscle most likely involved with the decreased ROM indicated by the client experiencing trouble extending the knee?

choices: Done the same as the strokes toward the heart

The heart lining wears away, opening the door to atherosclerosis inside the chambers
The heart muscle wears out, becomes distended and thin, and cannot pump enough blood to meet daily needs
The heart valves degenerate and allow backflow, mixing oxygenated and deoxygenated blood
questions.correctAnswer: The heart muscle wears out, becomes distended and thin, and cannot pump enough blood to meet daily needs

questions.question: Your client gets colds so frequently that he is in trouble for missing a lot of work. He wonders if receiving massage therapy might strengthen his immune system enough that he can have better resistance. What is your best option?
choices: Give him a challenging, rigorous massage session, and recommend that he take a sauna or soak in a hot tub later because these have been seen to detoxify the body

Inform him that massage therapy might help with immune system function for some people, but that you can’t promise it will help him be sick less often—however, he might feel better in general if he got massage on a regular basis
Work with him within his capacity for adaptation, and add immune-strengthening essential oils to your lotion
Assure him that regular massage therapy will help him with this goal, and make a list of supplements he should use to maximize that effect
questions.correctAnswer: Inform him that massage therapy might help with immune system function for some people, but that you can’t promise it will help him be sick less often—however, he might feel better in general if he got massage on a regular basis

Jose Campos has come to his massage therapist complaining of a shoulder issue.  When working under his car, he cannot raise his right arm above his head without great pain in his shoulder.

Upon assessment, Jose could not lift his right arm to the side away from his body without pain and his movement was very choppy with limited ROM compared to the left.  In addition, he held his right arm very close to his body, as if protecting his arm.

Describe the Postural issue:
choices: Lateral Rotation of the right glenohumeral joint
Flexion of the right scapulothoracic joint
Abduction of the right glenohumeral joint
Adduction of the right glenohumeral joint

choices: Massage therapy has been demonstrated to have a positive impact on menstrual cramps, if sessions occur at least once a week

Massage therapy can be helpful for people with menstrual cramps, and might even improve the situation so that she has less pain during her period, but extreme pain during menstruation is a good reason to visit her doctor

questions.question: How does hypertension lead to heart failure?

Pushing against resistance in the arteries causes the muscle fibers in the heart to lose elasticity and strength

choices: Work with him carefully within his limits for frailty and resilience; offer to teach his friends or family some simple and safe techniques for comfort
Work with him either wearing gloves or through the sheet, and consider wearing a face mask to reduce the risk of your exposure to his infection

Don’t do any hands-on massage, but offer to do hands-off work while gathered with his family and friends for a guided meditation
questions.correctAnswer: Work with him carefully within his limits for frailty and resilience; offer to teach his friends or family some simple and safe techniques for comfort

questions.question:
A 45-year-old female client who has a history of chronic neck problems and also a habitual left side sleeper is seeking to get some relief from the tension and stiffness in her neck.

The therapist observes that the client's neck has a slight rotation towards the left side. Upon taking her through a range of motion assessment, there was an obvious limited movement in rotation of the head and neck to the right. The client also stated a feeling of pulling and tension on the right side of her neck while performing the movement to the right.

What is the client's postural deviation?
choices: Rotation of the neck to the right
Left lateral flexion of the neck
Rotation of the neck to the left
questions.correctAnswer: Rotation of the neck to the left

choices: Welcome her and work with her to achieve comfort on the table and to establish her preferred level of pressure
Welcome her, give her a light massage, and provide referrals to diet and nutrition resources that appear to meet her needs

questions.correctAnswer: Welcome her and work with her to achieve comfort on the table and to establish her preferred level of pressure
